name: CircuitPython (Python for Beginners on Adafruit Hardware)

performance.notes: Similar to MicroPython; slightly slower due to abstraction layer

agent_readiness.notes: MicroPython fork optimized for beginners and Adafruit hardware. import board, digitalio, analogio, busio, pwmio. led = digitalio.DigitalInOut(board.LED); led.direction = digitalio.Direction.OUTPUT; led.value = True. ADC: adc = analogio.AnalogIn(board.A0); print(adc.value). I2C: i2c = busio.I2C(board.SCL, board.SDA). USB-as-drive: board shows as CIRCUITPY USB drive — drag and drop code.py. Adafruit libraries: adafruit_neopixel, adafruit_display_text, adafruit_ssd1306. Adafruit CircuitPython Bundle: 200+ libraries. asyncio in CP 9. Displays: displayio for e-ink, TFT, OLED. NeoPixel: import neopixel; pixels = neopixel.NeoPixel(board.NEOPIXEL, 10). Boards: Adafruit Feather, QT Py, CLUE, Trinket, etc. Excellent beginner documentation.

gotchas: Adafruit hardware focus — some non-Adafruit boards have limited support
Slightly different from MicroPython — not always compatible
Library versions must match CircuitPython version
CIRCUITPY drive can corrupt if unplugged during write
No asyncio in older versions (added in CP 9)
best_when: Beginners learning embedded Python with excellent docs
Adafruit hardware (Feather, QT Py, CLUE, etc.)
Drag-and-drop code editing without IDE setup
avoid_when: Non-Adafruit boards with better MicroPython support
When MicroPython's broader board support is needed
summary: CircuitPython is Adafruit's beginner-friendly embedded Python (MicroPython fork). board, digitalio, analogio, busio. USB drive as code editor — drag/drop code.py. 200+ Adafruit libraries. NeoPixel, displays, sensors. asyncio in CP 9. MIT by Adafruit. Excellent documentation. Best for beginners and Adafruit hardware. MicroPython for broader board support and more Pythonic async patterns.
